title: cerinthe minor subsp. auriculata
intro: Cerinthe minor subsp. auriculata, also known as Honeywort, is a flowering plant that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory problems, soothing digestive issues, and supporting the immune system. Honeywort can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
